title: Spoken language processing activates the primary visual cortex.

assets.abstract: Primary visual cortex (V1) is generally thought of as a low-level sensory area that primarily processes basic visual features. Although there is evidence for multisensory effects on its activity, these are typically found for the processing of simple sounds and their properties, for example spatially or temporally-congruent simple sounds. However, in congenitally blind individuals, V1 is involved in language processing, with no evidence of major changes in anatomical connectivity that could expl

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.
